note: golden_hash filled by golden-parity.test.mjs --update. Vector 1 reproduces tools/141-cross-border-b2b-fee-calculator.html default seed inputs exactly (US->GB, reverse-charge VAT rate 0, tool<->kernel parity). Vector 2: DEFAULT (non-treaty) VAT rule at 10% plus a documentary letter-of-credit cost. Vector 3: zero-invoice edge (finite gate).

vectors.name: default_seed_us_gb_reverse_charge
vectors.description: 85000 invoice, US->GB (VAT_RULES reverse-charge treaty, rate 0), wire_swift correspondent fee 35, 75bps FX spread, no documentary credit, 12 reconciliation cost -- reproduces tools/141 default seed exactly: fx_cost 637.50, total_cost 684.50, pct_of_invoice 0.8053%.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: VAT/reverse-charge treatment, documentary-credit fees, and correspondent/method fees are caller-supplied per their own treaty position and banking agreement -- this kernel never vendors a VAT-treaty or correspondent-fee table, only the published cost-stack formula.
output_payload.disambiguation: Itemizes one cross-border B2B invoice cost stack. For remittance-corridor benchmarking against World Bank RPW / SDG 10.c targets, see compare_corridor_cost.

vectors.name: default_vat_with_doc_credit
vectors.description: US->JP has no treaty entry so falls to VAT_RULES.DEFAULT (10% standard import VAT), plus an lc_sight documentary letter-of-credit cost of 1500.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: VAT/reverse-charge treatment, documentary-credit fees, and correspondent/method fees are caller-supplied per their own treaty position and banking agreement -- this kernel never vendors a VAT-treaty or correspondent-fee table, only the published cost-stack formula.
output_payload.disambiguation: Itemizes one cross-border B2B invoice cost stack. For remittance-corridor benchmarking against World Bank RPW / SDG 10.c targets, see compare_corridor_cost.

vectors.name: zero_invoice_edge
vectors.description: Zero invoice amount -- pct_of_invoice short-circuits to 0 instead of dividing by zero, and the non-positive-invoice compliance flag fires.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: VAT/reverse-charge treatment, documentary-credit fees, and correspondent/method fees are caller-supplied per their own treaty position and banking agreement -- this kernel never vendors a VAT-treaty or correspondent-fee table, only the published cost-stack formula.
output_payload.disambiguation: Itemizes one cross-border B2B invoice cost stack. For remittance-corridor benchmarking against World Bank RPW / SDG 10.c targets, see compare_corridor_cost.
